source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A cube (on the right) is colored in three colors so that each face has exactly one color and the opposite face has the same color. Which of the following developments is the development of this cube?
<image1>
<image2>
extraction_notes: options field contains only option labels without textual option content
question explicitly depends on images ('on the right', developments, <image1>, <image2>)

issues: metadata.extraction_notes indicates the options field contains only option labels without textual option content

image_dependency: question asks which cube development/net matches a coloring pattern, which is inherently visual/spatial
choices contain only labels A-E without textual option content, so the actual alternatives must be in the image
multi_step: solving requires combining the rule 'opposite face has the same color' with analysis of candidate cube nets
the task is multiple-choice over 5 visual candidates, implying comparison/elimination across several configurations
cube-net validity and opposite-face consistency typically require multi-step spatial reasoning rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is option and answer_text is a single explicit choice: 'E'
the correct option can in principle be checked against the provided image, though the text manifest alone is insufficient because option contents are not transcribed

Since resolution depends on the image and the textual extraction is incomplete, the sample is better kept in the pool at lower priority rather than rejected.
